claimText: Named after Arjuna, the main protagonist from the ancient Indian epic Mahabharata, the Arjuna Award was instituted in 1961. It was India's highest sporting honour before the Khel Ratna came into being.
evidenceExcerpt: Named after Arjuna, the main protagonist from the ancient Indian epic Mahabharata, the Arjuna Award was instituted in 1961. It was India's highest sporting honour before the Khel Ratna came into being.
examUse: Could support a statement-based or institution-feature question.

claimText: It is awarded for consistent good performance over a period of four years. The winners of the Arjuna Award receive a statuette of Arjuna, a certificate and a cash prize.
evidenceExcerpt: It is awarded for consistent good performance over a period of four years. The winners of the Arjuna Award receive a statuette of Arjuna, a certificate and a cash prize.
examUse: Could support a statement-based or institution-feature question.

claimText: The first Arjuna awards was conferred upon India's football Olympian PK Banerjee who was among the 20 recipients of the Arjuna Award in its inaugural edition in 1961.
evidenceExcerpt: The first Arjuna awards was conferred upon India's football Olympian PK Banerjee who was among the 20 recipients of the Arjuna Award in its inaugural edition in 1961.

claimText: Hockey player Anna Lumsden was the first woman Arjuna Award winner.
